data.title: Lyell Immunopharma gets FDA RMAT status for lymphoma treatment
data.description:
• Lyell Immunopharma (NASDAQ:LYEL) said on Tuesday that the U.S. FDA granted Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy status to LYL314 for the treatment of large B-cell lymphoma.
  • Stock rose about 9% in morning trade.
  • The RMAT designation was granted based on data from the ongoing early-stage trial of LYL314 in patients with relapsed and/or refractory large B-cell lymphoma which had an overall response rate of 94%, while 71% of patients achieved a complete response by three months.
  • Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy designation entails benefits such as early interactions with the FDA to discuss surrogate or intermediate endpoints that could support the accelerated approval and meet post-approval requirements.

data.title: Short sellers boost bets against S&P 500 industrial stocks for the second month
data.description:
Short sellers boosted their bets against the S&P 500 industrial sector stocks in March, with the S&P 500 Industrial ETF (NYSEARCA: XLI) down for the second month, losing almost 3.9%.

As of March's end, the average short interest rate in S&P 500 industrials rose to 2.15%, up 4 bps from 2.11% at the end of February.

The S&P 500's industrial sector (NYSEARCA: XLI) lost 3.9% in March, compared to the broader S&P 500, which was down 5.9%.

Short interest, which could be an indicator of pessimism, calculates the number of shares sold against the company float. (Short Interest % = Number of Shares Sold Short ÷ Stock Float).

Most shorted stocks:

United Airlines Holding (UAL) - 5.95% vs 4.09% last month.

Southwest Airlines (LUV) - 5.84% vs. 5.50%.

Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) - 5.08% vs. 4.33%.

Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) - 4.92% VS 5.09%.

Dayforce (DAY) - 4.88% VS 5.62%.

Least shorted stocks:

RTX Corporation (RTX) - 0.69% vs 0.83% last month.

Waste Management (WM) - 0.94% vs 0.93%.

Parker-Hannifin (PH) - 0.99% vs 0.88%.

AMETEK (AME) - 0.99% vs 0.97%.

Broadridge Financial Solutions (BR) - 1.00% vs 0.89%.

Within the industry, Passenger Airlines remained the most shorted industry within the industrial sector, with 5.03% short interest at the end of March, compared to 5.03% last month. Software were the second most shorted industry with a short interest of 5.00% compared to 5.38% at the end of February. Environmental and Facilities Services is the least shorted sector with short interest of 1.30% at the end of March.

data.title: Airbus reaffirms 2025 targets amid trade disruptions, Spirit AeroSystems deal
data.description:
Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) confirmed it is maintaining its full-year financial outlook for 2025, while cautioning that it is actively watching recent global trade and economic developments that could affect its business. The update came during the company’s annual shareholder meeting on Tuesday.

Chief Executive Guillaume Faury reiterated that the company’s previously issued guidance didn’t include the potential effect of tariffs, which at the time of the February announcement were still mostly unknown.

Since then, the trade environment has shifted rapidly, with the United States introducing a series of new import tariffs. Though some of those levies have been temporarily suspended for many countries, the European Union is expected to respond with countermeasures, creating uncertainty for cross-border aircraft deliveries and procurement.

Aerospace investors are now scrutinizing how trade policy may affect manufacturers such as Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) and rival Boeing (NYSE:BA), particularly as deliveries and orders could be disrupted in both directions across the Atlantic.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) is one of several industry players whose 2025 forecasts didn’t factor in potential tariff costs, meaning upcoming quarterly results may offer new insight into how companies plan to adapt.

In parallel with trade tensions, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) is continuing to deal with production challenges linked to persistent labor shortages and supply chain difficulties stemming from the pandemic.

Splitting Spirit AeroSystems with Boeing

Faury cited ongoing supply problems from U.S. supplier Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR), whose component delays are affecting output of Airbus’s A350 and A220 aircraft.

To help address those issues, Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) is moving forward with a planned acquisition of certain Spirit AeroSystems (NYSE:SPR) assets.

The company expects to finalize an agreement by the end of April and aims to complete the transaction by mid-year, with June 30 as the target date, said Chief Financial Officer Thomas Toepfer.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) has been in talks for nearly a year to assume control of work done at four Spirit facilities, as part of a broader divestiture of the supplier’s operations in a rare collaboration with rival Boeing (NYSE:BA), which is taking over other parts of Spirit’s (SPR) business.

Airbus (OTCPK:EADSF) (OTCPK:EADSY) continues to target the integration of these activities within its production system over the coming months. Faury, who is set to begin his third term as CEO, said the company remains focused on executing its industrial and financial plans despite shifts in the market landscape.

data.title: Coty falls after BofA flips to being a bear after being a bull
data.description:
Coty (NYSE:COTY) swung lower on Tuesday after Bank of America lowered its rating on the beauty products stock to Underperform in a two-notch downgrade from a Buy rating.

The firm said that it appreciates that Coty (NYSE:COTY) has a leading market position as a maker of fragrances and beauty products globally, but sees various challenges ahead.

Analyst Anna Lizzul noted that global momentum is slowing for Coty (COTY) in the prestige category and expects for the company to see category headwinds in consumer beauty due to broad concerns about broader global economic weakness.

"While we appreciate Coty’s efforts to build out its owned brands with Lancaster and Infiniment Coty Paris, we now believe these launches are unlikely to drive significant market share gains in their respective categories."

Looking ahead, Coty (CPTY) is seen having difficulties expanding into skincare and China. The potential for rising input costs is also seen as a threat to Coty's (CTOY) margins. 

Bank of America lowered its price objective on Coty (COTY) to $4.50 from $9.00 prior.

Shares of Coty (COTY) were down 7.7% in Tuesday morning trading to $4.79 and are swapping hands, which is not far off from the 52-week low of $4.61.

data.title: JPMorgan credit card net charge-off rate drifts up, delinquencies edge down in March
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) posted Tuesday mixed credit metrics as net charge-offs continued to rise while delinquencies turned lower, according to a regulatory filing. Both rates stayed comfortably below their prepandemic levels.

The Chase Issuance Trust's net charge off rate of 1.85% rose from 1.68% in February and 1.49% a year ago. It remains well below the prepandemic mark of 2.55% in March 2019.

The trust's delinquency rate was 0.89% in March, down from 0.91% in February and up from 0.79% a year earlier. That compares with 1.23% five years before.

Meanwhile, credit card lending ticked up to $12.2B in March from $12.1B in February, the Securities and Exchange Commission filing showed.

data.title: Canada throws lifeline to automakers, Canadian businesses reliant on U.S. inputs
data.description:
Canada is offering a lifeline for domestic businesses that rely on U.S.-imported inputs, as well as for the U.S. automotive industry, that would incentivize Canadian manufacturing and offer relief to consumers faced with higher prices for everything from cars to bottled beverages, to bandages.

These measures include the remission of some countermeasure tariffs announced by Canada “in response to unjustified tariffs imposed by the U.S. on Canadian products,” said Canadian finance minister Francois-Philippe Champagne.

Under the proposal, automakers who manufacture cars in Canada can continue to import a certain number of U.S.-assembled USMCA-compliant vehicles into Canada, tariff-free, contingent on these automakers continuing to produce vehicles in Canada and on completing planned investments. However, the number of tariff-free vehicles a company is permitted to import will be reduced if there are reductions in Canadian production or investment.

The measure is aimed at encouraging auto giants like General Motors (NYSE:GM), Ford (F), Stellantis (NYSE:STLA), Honda (NYSE:HMC), and Toyota (NYSE:TM) to keep manufacturing facilities in the country in defiance of Washington's efforts to bring manufacturing back to the U.S.

The proposal also offers a temporary reprieve to Canadian companies that import U.S.-made goods that are used in manufacturing, processing, and food and beverage packaging, as well as those used to support public health, health care, public safety, and national security objectives. This very broad interpretation would exempt tariffs for 6-months in an effort to give Canadian businesses time to reshore manufacturing and/or prioritize domestic sources of supply.

Finally, the Large Enterprise Tariff Loan Facility provides financing to Canadian businesses that contribute to the food supply, energy security, economic security, and national security to sustain operations.

data.title: Bank of America credit card charge-offs rise in March
data.description:
Bank of America's (NYSE:BAC) credit card delinquency rate fell in March, while its net charge-off rate increased, according to a filing by its credit card master trust.

The BA Master Credit Card Trust II's delinquency rate fell to 1.47% from 1.51% in February. That increased from 1.41% a year ago, but remains below the 1.67% rate in March 2019, before the pandemic disrupted credit dynamics.

The trust's net charge-off rate of 2.76% increased from 2.50% in February and 2.46% a year ago. It's now approaching its March 2019 level of 2.82%.

Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) appears to be more cautious in lending. The trust's receivables outstanding were $15.1B, down 0.7% from February and 5.0% from March 2024, according to the filing.

data.title: Eos Energy enters U.K. energy storage market via Frontier Power partnership
data.description:
Eos Energy Enterprises (NASDAQ:EOSE) +15.8% in Tuesday's trading after saying it signed a memorandum of understanding with U.K.-based Frontier Power for a 5 GWh energy storage framework agreement.

Eos Energy (NASDAQ:EOSE) said the agreement marks its entrance into a new international market and supports Frontier’s plans to submit multiple bids utilizing Eos' Znyth battery technology in the first application window of Ofgem's new long-duration energy storage cap and floor scheme.

The companies also said they will look to expand the collaboration globally to new international markets, possibly opening the door to developing local manufacturing in the U.K.; if significant LDES project volumes materialize using Eos (EOSE) technology, it could incentivize the establishment of manufacturing operations in the U.K., the companies said.

"The novel cap and floor scheme incentivizes investments in long-duration storage technologies that are critical for grid stability and renewable integration," Eos Energy (EOSE) Senior VP of Global Sales Justin Vagnozzi said.

data.title: Verve extends gains as Cantor Fitzgerald upgrades on data for cholesterol drug
data.description:
Shares of Verve Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VERV) climbed for the second straight session on Tuesday after Cantor Fitzgerald upgraded the stock based on early-stage trial data the gene editing company posted for its cholesterol therapy VERVE-102.

Verve (NASDAQ:VERV) closed 26% higher on Monday after the readout, which included initial data from its Heart-2 Phase 1b trial for VERVE-102 for two patient groups characterized by high levels of LDL-C, also known as bad cholesterol.

Cantor analyst Rick Bienkowski upgraded VERV to Overweight from Neutral, arguing that Heart-2 data, which represented three dose levels of VERVE-102, exceeded the firm’s expectations for the one-time therapy in terms of both safety and efficacy.

Bienkowski pointed out that the readout didn’t indicate any treatment-related adverse events or any clinically significant changes in liver enzymes ALT, AST, or platelets.

Arguing that there appeared to be no relationship between dose responses and any measure of safety, Bienkowski said that this could be “a positive indicator for safety in the ongoing higher dose cohort (0.7 mg/kg) and the potential switch to fixed doses of VERVE-102 in Phase 2 trials.”

Citing the 53% LDL-C reduction seen in the study’s 0.6 mg/kg dose cohort, the analyst added that VERVE-102 could be "best-in-class for achieving LDL-C goals in heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HeFH),” a genetic condition linked to high cholesterol.

data.title: Ingersoll-Rand acquired G&D Chillers, Advanced Gas for $27M
data.description:
• Ingersoll Rand (NYSE:IR) has acquired G & D Chillers and Advanced Gas Technologies for a combined purchase price of ~$27M to further grow the company’s air treatment portfolio.
  • The execution of bolt-on acquisition strategy continues to enhance the company’s durable financial profile by adding highly complementary products and capabilities focused on high-growth, sustainable end markets.
  • These acquisitions will expand Ingersoll Rand’s product and technology portfolio with additional chiller and onsite gas generation offerings and support the company’s in-region for the region strategy.
  • The acquisitions have a combined pre-synergy Adjusted EBITDA purchase multiple of high-single digits.
